title: The One With The Morning After

jokes.text: What do you want to drink? Who's that? Nobody. Is that Mark?
jokes.setup: Ross tries to have a reconciliation conversation with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Mark immediately shows up, creating maximum awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and dramatic irony - the audience knows this will complicate everything. Strong structure with rapid-fire questions building tension.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, we need to talk. I've been thinking about us, and I really think we should—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, I appreciate that, but—

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: Hey Rachel! Sorry I'm early, I was just in the neighborhood.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mark. Of course. Mark is here.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh! Um, Mark, hi. This is... this is actually not a great time.

dialogue.speaker: Mark
dialogue.line: Oh, I can come back later if you guys are—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, stay. Why not? Everyone else does.

jokes.text: Hey, come dance. What, are you married? Because that's okay.
jokes.setup: Someone at the party approaches a woman to dance
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions marriage isn't a dealbreaker for dancing (implying hookups)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. Good economy but limited character development since it's a throwaway character.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown party guest
dialogue.line: Hey, you wanna dance?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: I'm married.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown party guest
dialogue.line: That's okay, we're just dancing.

jokes.text: Nice. No, we kind of broke up instead.
jokes.setup: Monica assumes 'we never got to dinner' means they had sex
jokes.punchline: Rachel deadpans that they broke up instead
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with Monica's assumption vs reality. Rachel's deadpan delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, so you never got to dinner?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, we broke up.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: My God, Monica! It's on the ceiling! That's okay. This is more important than fruit on my ceiling.
jokes.setup: Something is stuck on Monica's ceiling
jokes.punchline: Monica dismisses fruit on the ceiling as less important than relationship drama
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual gag that perfectly captures Monica's priorities shifting from cleanliness to friendship. Character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, there's fruit on your ceiling.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How did fruit get on your ceiling?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't want to talk about it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, I'm going through something right now. The fruit on my ceiling is the least of my problems.

jokes.text: You sure you need shoes?
jokes.setup: Ross is rushing Chloe out before Rachel arrives
jokes.punchline: He's so desperate he suggests she leave barefoot
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's panic and poor decision-making under pressure. The desperation is palpable and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You have to go. Rachel's going to be here any minute.

dialogue.speaker: Chloe
dialogue.line: But I can't find my shoes.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't care! Just go! Go barefoot!

jokes.text: You must be so happy! Yes, I am. One of the many things I'm feeling.
jokes.setup: Chloe innocently congratulates Ross on getting back with his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated response reveals his internal turmoil
jokes.explanation: Excellent understated delivery that captures Ross's guilt and complexity. The irony is perfectly earned through the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Chloe
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Ross! I heard you got back together with your girlfriend! That's so great!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah... great.

jokes.text: I have the spirit of an old Indian woman living in mine.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions banana on her ceiling
jokes.punchline: Phoebe one-ups with her typical mystical nonsense
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe - completely random, spiritually absurd, and delivered with total sincerity. Could only come from this character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that's nothing. I once had a whole fruit salad appear on my ceiling. It was a sign from my past lives telling me I needed more potassium.

jokes.text: So then you know.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions the spirit in her ceiling
jokes.punchline: Monica treats this supernatural claim as perfectly logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Monica's deadpan acceptance of Phoebe's absurdity is beautifully economical.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: There's a spirit in my ceiling.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, that explains the noises. Have you tried asking it to leave?

jokes.text: Hello! Organic substances recently discovered in the depths of the rain forest? They have the best stuff in there.
jokes.setup: Discussion about painless wax products
jokes.punchline: Monica buys into infomercial pseudo-science completely
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of infomercial language and Monica's gullibility, but feels a bit drawn out for the payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, a painless wax? That's impossible!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It says right here it's clinically proven.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Clinically proven? Well, that changes everything! I'm ordering ten!

jokes.text: How dumb are you?
jokes.setup: Ross asks about addressing 'the more important question'
jokes.punchline: Chandler cuts straight to the real issue - Ross's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - cuts through Ross's self-important rambling with brutal efficiency. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What's the more important question?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How did you get so stupid?

jokes.text: And that's what deathbeds are for.
jokes.setup: Discussion about when to tell Rachel the truth
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests waiting until literally dying
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and perfectly Joey - treating deathbed confessions as practical relationship advice. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, maybe we should wait until she's dying to tell her. You know, like on her deathbed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's your answer to everything, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? It works in the movies!

jokes.text: You always have to think about the trail!
jokes.setup: Chandler explains how secrets spread through networks
jokes.punchline: His dramatic emphasis on 'the trail' like it's a known principle
jokes.explanation: Chandler treating adultery like a detective procedural. Good character voice and situational comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: If you tell a secret to one person, they tell two people, and those two people tell four people, and so on and so on. Before you know it, the whole world knows. It's exponential. It's like a virus. And there's no stopping it once it's out there. The trail is unstoppable.

jokes.text: And Phoebe's friends with Rachel, and that's the trail! I did it!
jokes.setup: Chandler traces the connection path
jokes.punchline: His proud 'I did it!' like he solved a puzzle
jokes.explanation: Chandler's detective enthusiasm is endearing and character-specific. Good payoff to the setup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I did it!

jokes.text: Oh, my God, they should call it 'Pain-zine, now with a little wax.'
jokes.setup: Rachel tries the 'pain-free' wax strips
jokes.punchline: She creates a more honest product name
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but not particularly clever. Functions as expected product parody.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, these are supposed to be pain-free wax strips. Let me just... oh my God! OW! OW! OW!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to rename these. From now on, they're called 'Liar Strips.'

jokes.text: That's because their nerves are deadened from being so stupid.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions girls in commercials don't seem to feel pain
jokes.punchline: Rachel explains it with an insult about their intelligence
jokes.explanation: Solid insult comedy with some wit, but not groundbreaking. Rachel's frustration comes through.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that's because they're not very bright.

jokes.text: Now aren't you glad we didn't start with the bikini strips?
jokes.setup: Monica experiences pain from leg waxing
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggests the alternative would be much worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the pain concept. Makes the audience imagine worse scenarios.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, the alternative would be much worse.

jokes.text: You took my watch? I'm sorry. I do that.
jokes.setup: Ross discovers Chloe took his watch
jokes.punchline: Chloe casually admits to being a kleptomaniac
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chloe's casual relationship with boundaries. Well-delivered surprise.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chloe, have you seen my watch? I left it right here.

dialogue.speaker: Chloe
dialogue.line: Oh, that. Yeah, I took it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You took it? Why would you just take my watch?

dialogue.speaker: Chloe
dialogue.line: I'm a kleptomaniac. I take things. It's kind of my thing.

jokes.text: You dog!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to have a serious conversation about discretion
jokes.punchline: Isaac treats it like Ross is bragging about a conquest
jokes.explanation: Shows the disconnect between Ross's guilt and Isaac's celebration. Creates good uncomfortable tension.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Isaac, I need to talk to you about something important. It's about discretion. What happens between me and Rachel needs to stay private.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Ross, I don't need to hear about your conquests, man. That's between you and her.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, you don't understand. I'm asking you NOT to tell anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: Oh sure, sure. Your secret's safe with me, buddy. You dog!

jokes.text: Monogamy is too cruel a rule.
jokes.setup: Isaac discusses relationships
jokes.punchline: He delivers pseudo-philosophical justification for cheating
jokes.explanation: Good character voice for the 'enlightened' guy who justifies bad behavior. Memorable phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: He delivers pseudo-philosophical justification for cheating

jokes.text: Actually, no, we're not. Yeah, we are. No, we're not. Yeah, we are. No, we're not. Okay, we're not. Right. But we are.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to differentiate himself from Isaac
jokes.punchline: They get stuck in an increasingly childish back-and-forth
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity that shows both characters' immaturity. Gets repetitive but builds well.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm nothing like Isaac Newton.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well I invented calculus.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I have a PhD in paleontology.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: I have a PhD in everything.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I'm taller than you.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: I'm deader than you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well... you smell.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: Your mom smells.

jokes.text: I can promise not to tell her again.
jokes.setup: Ross asks Isaac not to tell his sister
jokes.punchline: Isaac reveals he already told her
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sounds like agreement but reveals the opposite. Good economy and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Please don't tell Monica about this.

dialogue.speaker: Isaac
dialogue.line: Oh, I already told her.

jokes.text: You did a bad thing! Yes, I did. Very bad! Very bad. Very, very bad! I'm agreeing with you.
jokes.setup: Jasmine scolds Ross for his behavior
jokes.punchline: Ross keeps agreeing while she escalates her condemnation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and Ross's resigned acceptance is character-appropriate. Shows his guilt spiral.

jokes.text: Was I not supposed to?
jokes.setup: Ross desperately asks if Gunther told Rachel about his affair
jokes.punchline: Gunther innocently asks if he wasn't supposed to tell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment - his oblivious delivery of devastating news. Excellent economy and character voice creates maximum impact.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You didn't tell Rachel, did you?

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Oh, was I not supposed to tell her?

jokes.text: We were just waxing our legs. Off?
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler hear screaming from the girls
jokes.punchline: Ross misunderstands 'waxing legs' as 'waxing legs off'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that works on the literal/figurative confusion. Ross's question is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Aahhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh God!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What happened? What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're waxing our legs!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You're waxing your legs off?!

jokes.text: I think women just have a lower threshold for pain than men, that's all. I mean, come on, it's just a little wax.
jokes.setup: Ross dismisses women's pain tolerance and the waxing experience
jokes.punchline: Sets himself up for immediate comeuppance
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for physical comedy payoff. Ross's arrogance makes the comeuppance satisfying.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How bad can waxing be? I mean, it's just removing hair.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, you want to try it?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure. How painful could it be?

dialogue.speaker: Esthetician
dialogue.line: Okay, let's do your chest.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Ah! Oh! Oh! Oh! NO! NO! STOP! STOP!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Not so bad, huh?

jokes.text: Okay, Tin Man.
jokes.setup: Ross gets wax strips applied to his legs
jokes.punchline: Monica calls him Tin Man, implying he's frozen in place
jokes.explanation: Great pop culture reference that perfectly captures the visual. Monica's timing and word choice are spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Tin Man!

jokes.text: A mistake? What were you trying to put it in? Her purse?
jokes.setup: Ross calls sleeping with another woman a 'mistake'
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically suggests anatomical confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crafted comeback that cuts through Ross's euphemism with surgical precision. Perfect economy and devastating delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I see. So you were just confused about which woman you were sleeping with?

jokes.text: Where did he put it?
jokes.setup: Monica's purse comment creates confusion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe takes the misdirection literally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her innocent confusion adds another layer to Monica's joke. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen my purse?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you said your *nurse*. I was gonna say, 'No, but I know a great acupuncturist.'

jokes.text: I knew something had to be wrong because my fingernails did not grow at all yesterday.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacts to news of Ross's affair
jokes.punchline: Phoebe connects it to her fingernail growth
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe absurdism - connecting cosmic/spiritual signs to mundane events. Could only come from this character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! How could you?!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, this explains so much. My fingernails have been growing really fast lately, and I read that means someone close to you is being unfaithful. I thought it was Monica!

jokes.text: He has sex and we get hit in our heads!
jokes.setup: Monica realizes they're suffering consequences for Ross's actions
jokes.punchline: Points out the unfair distribution of karma
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about unfair consequences. Monica's indignation is perfectly justified and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is so unfair! Ross does something stupid and WE all suffer the consequences. Where's his karma? Why do we get punished for his mistakes?

jokes.text: Okay. All right. How was she? What? Was she good? Don't answer that.
jokes.setup: Rachel says she wants to talk about the affair
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks then forbids the most painful question
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the self-torture of wanting to know but not wanting to know. Rachel's emotional conflict is painfully relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I want to talk about the affair.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Did you... no, don't tell me.

jokes.text: She was different. Good different? Nobody likes change.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to diplomatically answer about the other woman
jokes.punchline: His attempt at neutrality just makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Ross's diplomatic disaster - his attempt to be kind just makes everything worse. Great cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So who is she?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, she's... she's smart, and she's funny, and she's...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no, I'm just saying she has qualities that are—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Qualities? Ross, those are the same things you said about me!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I mean... she's different qualities. Different smart. Different funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, so I'm not smart anymore?

jokes.text: Should we do something? Yeah. Never cheat on Rachel.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if they should intervene in the fight
jokes.punchline: Chandler draws the obvious lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler observation - practical wisdom delivered with his characteristic dryness. Excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Should we do something?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, let's break it up. I'll take the tall one.

jokes.text: You sure had a hell of a time at the wake.
jokes.setup: Ross says he thought their relationship was dead
jokes.punchline: Rachel extends the metaphor to mock his behavior
jokes.explanation: Devastating metaphor extension that perfectly captures Rachel's anger and wit. One of the episode's strongest lines - quotable and emotionally earned.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you spent less time with the corpse and more time with me, it wouldn't be dead!

jokes.text: I think I'm falling in love with you all over again.
jokes.setup: Ross explains he ran around trying to keep the secret
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically 'swoons' at his deception efforts
jokes.explanation: Rachel's sarcasm cuts deep - treating his cover-up efforts as romantic gestures. Perfect delivery of devastating irony.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh wow, you ran around trying to keep a secret? That's so romantic. I'm swooning.

jokes.text: Wax the door shut. We're never leaving.
jokes.setup: Ross throws Chandler and Joey under the bus
jokes.punchline: Monica suggests permanent imprisonment using their wax
jokes.explanation: Good callback that combines the physical comedy subplot with the emotional drama. Creative solution to their predicament.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We should just keep them in the wax museum. Problem solved.

jokes.text: We could eat the wax! It's organic! Oh, great. Food with hair on it.
jokes.setup: They're trapped and getting hungry
jokes.punchline: Phoebe points out the unappetizing reality of used wax
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the absurdity while creating a gross-out visual. Phoebe's practicality is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're trapped in here. We're going to starve.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, we could always eat the wax.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's disgusting.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What? It's organic. Plus, it's already been used.

jokes.text: No, not the used wax. Because that would be crazy?
jokes.setup: Monica clarifies she meant unused wax
jokes.punchline: Phoebe questions where the line for 'crazy' is drawn
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - questioning arbitrary boundaries of sanity. Good escalation of the absurd situation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I meant unused wax.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, where do you draw the line?

jokes.text: No anchovies. With extra anchovies. That's okay. I'll pick them off. And could you please chop some up and just put it right in the sauce?
jokes.setup: Rachel orders pizza and says no anchovies
jokes.punchline: Monica escalates to having anchovies mixed into the sauce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the pettiness of their trapped situation. Monica's passive-aggressive food sabotage is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No anchovies.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm going to have them mix the anchovies into the sauce.

jokes.text: You can have the last piece if you want. Well, I should think so. You slept with someone.
jokes.setup: Rachel offers Ross the last piece of pizza
jokes.punchline: She treats it as inadequate compensation for adultery
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that treats massive betrayal as minor pizza etiquette. Rachel's proportional response is brilliantly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You can have the last piece of pizza.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, we need to talk about what happened with the copy girl.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, so one piece of pizza makes it okay that you slept with someone else?

jokes.text: What if they don't? You think I need a new walk?
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Ross and Rachel will work it out
jokes.punchline: Joey completely changes subject to his walking style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey randomness - his self-absorption during serious moments. Good character consistency and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, man. Do you think Ross and Rachel are actually going to make it work this time?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I think they've got a real shot. I mean, they've been through so much together.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's true. Maybe this time will be different.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, do you notice something different about the way I've been walking lately?

jokes.text: I think I need a take-notice walk. Are you actually saying these words?
jokes.setup: Joey explains his desire for a more impressive walk
jokes.punchline: Chandler questions the reality of this conversation
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler reaction to Joey's absurdity. His disbelief perfectly captures audience reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I want to have a walk that's like, you know, really impressive. Like when I walk into a room, people go 'Whoa!'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, no one's going 'Whoa' when you walk into a room.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, maybe not yet. But I'm working on it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could you BE any more delusional?

jokes.text: I don't cheat, right? That's not me. I'm not Joey! Yeah, okay.
jokes.setup: Ross protests that cheating isn't in his character
jokes.punchline: He uses Joey as the example of a cheater, with Joey right there
jokes.explanation: Good character humor with Ross's tactless comparison and Joey's resigned acceptance. Well-established character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I would never cheat. That's not who I am.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What about me?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You? You cheat on people all the time!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey!

jokes.text: You notice how neither one of them are wondering where I am? Yeah, you know, people can be so self-involved.
jokes.setup: Joey complains that Ross and Rachel aren't worried about him
jokes.punchline: Chandler agrees while Joey demonstrates the same self-involvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony with Joey being self-involved while complaining about self-involvement. Chandler's deadpan agreement enhances the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: They don't care about me! They're not worried about me at all!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're right, they don't.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, what if something happened to me? Would they even notice?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Probably not for a while.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: This is serious! I could disappear and nobody would care!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'd care.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, not really.

jokes.text: Is that your new walk? No. I really have to pee.
jokes.setup: Joey walks unusually after the serious conversation
jokes.punchline: His 'cool walk' is just physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good callback that deflates Joey's earlier pretensions with simple physical reality. Nice bookend to the subplot.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why are you walking like that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? This is my cool walk.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not a cool walk, that's a cry for help.

tmdb_overview: Ross tries to prevent Rachel from learning his disturbing secret. She finds out anyway and they go back to her apartment to have a fight. The other four friends are trapped in Monica's bedroom, forced to listen in.
insight: Rachel's jealousy spiral drives 85-rated episode with 45 jokes in tight emotional framework.
